seoTitle: How to Change or Revise Buyer Feedback on eBay in 2025
metaDescription: Learn how buyers and sellers can revise feedback on eBay, including sending a revision request from Seller Hub and the time limits that apply.
intro: Feedback mistakes happen, whether you left a rating too quickly as a buyer or need a revision as a seller after resolving an issue. This guide covers both sides: how buyers respond to a seller's revision request, and how sellers send one through Seller Hub. It also explains the time limits eBay places on feedback revisions and what happens if no action is taken.

steps.heading: As a buyer, respond to a revision request
steps.body: If a seller sends you a feedback revision request, review it and decide whether to update the feedback you originally left.

steps.heading: As a seller, send a feedback revision request
steps.body: Open your Seller Hub and send a feedback revision request to the buyer, especially useful after resolving a dispute or shipping delay.

steps.heading: Know eBay's time limits and rules
steps.body: Be aware that eBay only allows a limited number of feedback revisions within a set time window, so act promptly once an issue is resolved.

steps.heading: Understand what happens with no response
steps.body: If the buyer doesn't respond to a revision request within the allowed time, the original feedback typically stays unchanged.

faq.question: Can a seller ask me to change feedback I already left?
faq.answer: Yes, sellers can send a feedback revision request through Seller Hub, which buyers can then choose to accept or ignore.

faq.question: Is there a time limit to revise feedback on eBay?
faq.answer: Yes, eBay only allows feedback revisions within a limited time window and caps how many revisions are permitted.

faq.question: What happens if I don't respond to a revision request?
faq.answer: If no action is taken within the allowed time, the original feedback generally remains unchanged.

faq.question: Can sellers change feedback on their own without buyer approval?
faq.answer: No, sellers can only request a revision; the buyer has to approve the change for it to take effect.
